lesson.subtitle: Aula 8 -- Remote Team Management -- Async Communication
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael Pelizaro | Aula 8 -- Remote Team Management

listenings.text: Hi team, this is Rafael with my async stand-up update for the end of my day. First, the good news: the login fix is complete and tested on staging. Now the handoff for the night team. There are two tasks left, and I wrote them clearly in the channel. Please deploy the fix when CI is green, and please keep all the reports in one thread, so we follow one topic. I want to be honest about one blocker today. Access to the test server was missing, so I asked the admin to fix it, and here is a heads-up: it should be ready in a few hours. Finally, two small requests in the thread. Could you read the handoff before you start, and share your availability for tomorrow? And if you have any question, would you mind looping in Marta? I am offline now, but I will read everything when I am back. Thank you, and have a good shift.

Hi Rafael, this is Marta with a quick voice message at the end of my shift. Good news first: the handoff is complete. I deployed the fix after CI was green, and everything is stable, no errors so far. I put all the details in the same thread, so the whole team can follow one topic when they wake up. I also read your heads-up about the test server, and the admin confirmed that the access is back. One small thing for you. When you start your day, could you review the thread and confirm the release plan? There is no rush before your morning. I shared my availability for tomorrow in the channel, and I looped in the product owner, just in case. Thanks for the clear handoff. It made the night much easier. Talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, say clearly what is done today. Next, write a clear handoff step for the next team, like please deploy the fix when CI is green. Then name any blocker and give the team a heads-up. After that, keep all the details in one thread, so everyone follows one topic. Finally, make a polite request and share your availability, for example could you confirm the plan when you start your day.
